entities.name: Badminton
entities.description: A sport played on a rectangular court with a net.

entities.name: Court
entities.description: The playing area in badminton.

entities.name: Net
entities.description: A horizontal bar suspended between the two sides of the court.

entities.name: Shuttlecock
entities.description: The conical projectile struck with a racket in badminton.

entities.name: Racket
entities.description: The implement used to hit the shuttlecock in badminton.

entities.name: Athletic Clothing
entities.description: Appropriate clothing worn by players during badminton matches.

entities.name: Badminton Shoes
entities.description: Footwear designed for playing badminton.

entities.name: Scoring System
entities.description: The system used to determine points in badminton.

entities.name: Rally Point Scoring
entities.description: A scoring method where a point is awarded for each rally.

entities.name: Match
entities.description: The overall competition in badminton, consisting of multiple games.

entities.name: Game
entities.description: A single unit of play in a match.

entities.name: Point
entities.description: The basic unit of scoring in badminton.

entities.name: Singles
entities.description: A match format where one player competes against another.

entities.name: Doubles
entities.description: A match format where two players compete against two other players.

entities.name: Service Court
entities.description: The area on the court from which a player serves.

entities.name: Badminton
entities.description: A sport played with racquets and shuttlecock.

entities.name: Service
entities.description: The act of serving the shuttlecock in badminton.

entities.name: Shuttlecock
entities.description: A small, feathered projectile used in badminton.

entities.name: Racket
entities.description: A tool used to hit the shuttlecock in badminton.

entities.name: Court
entities.description: The playing area for badminton.

entities.name: Net
entities.description: A horizontal bar used to separate the two halves of a badminton court.

entities.name: Service Line
entities.description: The line on the court where service must be made from.

entities.name: Center Line
entities.description: The central dividing line of the badminton court.

entities.name: Sideline
entities.description: The lines on either side of the badminton court.

entities.name: Back Boundary Line
entities.description: The line at the back of the badminton court.

entities.name: Let
entities.description: A situation where the rally is stopped and replayed without a point being scored.

entities.name: Umpire
entities.description: The official who makes decisions during the game.

entities.name: Shuttle
entities.description: The small, lightweight birdie used in badminton.

entities.name: Service court
entities.description: The designated area where the serve is made.

entities.name: Fault
entities.description: An error in badminton that results in a point loss or serve change.

entities.name: Shuttlecock
entities.description: The small, lightweight birdie used in badminton.

entities.name: Net
entities.description: The horizontal bar that separates the two sides of a badminton court.

entities.name: Court boundaries
entities.description: The lines defining the playing area in badminton.

entities.name: Service court
entities.description: One of two designated areas on a badminton court where serves must be made from.

entities.name: Service faults
entities.description: A specific category of faults that occur during the serve in badminton.

entities.name: Hitting the shuttle above the waist
entities.description: The entire shuttle must be below 1.15 meters from the surface of the court at the instant of being hit.

entities.name: Not keeping both feet on the ground
entities.description: The server must have both feet on the ground at the moment of contact.

entities.name: Not hitting the shuttle in an upward direction
entities.description: The shaft of the racket must be pointing downward at the moment of contact, and the shuttle must be hit in an upward direction.

entities.name: Serving from the wrong service court
entities.description: The server must serve from the correct service court based on the score.

entities.name: Not serving diagonally
entities.description: The serve must cross the net and land in the opponent's service court.

entities.name: The receiver not being ready
entities.description: If the server serves before the receiver is ready, it is a let, not a fault.

entities.name: The server taking too long
entities.description: The server must serve within 5 seconds after the server and receiver are ready.

entities.name: The server's partner interfering
entities.description: In doubles, the server's partner must not interfere with the receiver's ability to see the shuttle.

relationships.target: Hitting the shuttle above the waist
Not keeping both feet on the ground
Not hitting the shuttle in an upward direction
Serving from the wrong service court

entities.name: Badminton
entities.description: A sport played with a shuttlecock and racquets.

entities.name: Continuous Play
entities.description: Players are expected to play continuously without delay.

entities.name: Medical Timeout
entities.description: A timeout allowed when a player is unable to continue due to injury or illness.

entities.name: Misconduct
entities.description: Behavior that is unsportsmanlike or disrespectful during the game.

entities.name: Umpire
entities.description: A person who officiates and enforces the rules of the game.

entities.name: Warning
entities.description: An action taken by the umpire to caution a player for misconduct.

entities.name: Fault
entities.description: An action taken by the umpire when a player commits an infraction.

entities.name: Disqualification
entities.description: The removal of a player from the match or tournament for misconduct.

entities.name: Spirit of the Game
entities.description: The values and principles that guide fair play and sportsmanship in badminton.
